---
title: "Proof and mathematical reasoning"
subtitle: "Companion worksheet"
author: "Wayward House"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    theme: none
    css: ../_extensions/wayward-book/wayward-book.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        body {
          padding: 2rem 0 4rem;
        }
        main.content {
          width: min(100% - 2rem, 900px);
          margin: 0 auto;
        }
        .worksheet-intro {
          border: 1px solid var(--wh-rule);
          background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--wh-paper-2) 45%, #fff);
          padding: 1rem 1.15rem;
          margin: 1.25rem 0 1.75rem;
        }
        .worksheet-intro p:last-child {
          margin-bottom: 0;
        }
        .worksheet-block {
          border: 1px solid var(--wh-rule);
          padding: 1rem 1rem 1.15rem;
          margin: 0 0 1.25rem;
          background: #fff;
          break-inside: avoid;
        }
        .worksheet-kicker {
          font-family: var(--wh-font-mono);
          font-size: 0.72rem;
          letter-spacing: 0.14em;
          text-transform: uppercase;
          color: var(--wh-text-3);
          margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
        }
        .worksheet-block h2 {
          font-family: var(--wh-font-display);
          font-size: clamp(1.8rem, 4vw, 2.4rem);
          line-height: 0.95;
          text-transform: uppercase;
          margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
        }
        .worksheet-prompt,
        .worksheet-meta,
        .worksheet-check {
          font-family: var(--wh-font-body);
        }
        .worksheet-meta {
          color: var(--wh-text-2);
          font-size: 0.96rem;
          margin-bottom: 0.9rem;
        }
        .worksheet-field {
          margin-top: 0.9rem;
        }
        .worksheet-field h3 {
          font-family: var(--wh-font-mono);
          font-size: 0.78rem;
          letter-spacing: 0.12em;
          text-transform: uppercase;
          color: var(--wh-text-3);
          margin: 0 0 0.45rem;
        }
        .worksheet-lines {
          min-height: 7rem;
          background-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent calc(1.85rem - 1px), var(--wh-rule) calc(1.85rem - 1px), var(--wh-rule) 1.85rem);
          background-size: 100% 1.85rem;
          border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--wh-rule) 80%, white);
        }
        .worksheet-lines--tight {
          min-height: 4.5rem;
        }
        .worksheet-lines--tall {
          min-height: 10.5rem;
        }
        .worksheet-grid {
          display: grid;
          grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
          gap: 0.85rem;
        }
        .worksheet-check {
          margin-top: 0.95rem;
          padding-top: 0.85rem;
          border-top: 1px dashed var(--wh-rule);
          color: var(--wh-text-2);
        }
        @media (max-width: 700px) {
          .worksheet-grid {
            grid-template-columns: 1fr;
          }
        }
        @media print {
          body {
            padding: 0;
            background: white;
          }
          .wayward-book-header,
          .wayward-book-footer,
          .back-to-top {
            display: none !important;
          }
          main.content {
            width: 100%;
            max-width: none;
            margin: 0;
          }
          .worksheet-block,
          .worksheet-intro {
            box-shadow: none;
          }
        }
        </style>
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry:
      - top=18mm
      - bottom=18mm
      - left=18mm
      - right=18mm
    colorlinks: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Use This Worksheet

::: {.worksheet-intro}
This companion sheet is built for handwritten work on an iPad or on paper. The point is not to copy the final polished proof immediately. The point is to force the structure of the reasoning into the open.

Try each problem in this order:

1. Decide what must be assumed and what must be shown.
2. Pick the proof method before you start writing.
3. Write the key definitions explicitly.
4. Draft the argument in the working space.
5. Rewrite the clean version in the final-proof box.

Use the chapter page only after attempting the proof yourself.
:::

## Exercise 1

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
Direct Proof
:::

### Divisibility proof

::: {.worksheet-meta}
Hint: write n as three times an integer, then square the expression.
:::

::: {.worksheet-grid}
::: {.worksheet-field}
### What Must Be Shown
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Key Definition
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Working
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Final Proof
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-check}
Self-check: where exactly did you use the definition of divisibility?
:::
:::

## Exercise 2

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
Contradiction
:::

### Logarithm irrationality proof

::: {.worksheet-meta}
Hint: assume the base-2 logarithm of 3 can be written as a reduced fraction, then convert that logarithm statement into an exponential one.
:::

::: {.worksheet-grid}
::: {.worksheet-field}
### Assumption For Contradiction
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Key Theorem Or Fact
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Working
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Final Proof
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-check}
Self-check: what makes the final equality impossible, and have you stated that reason explicitly?
:::
:::

## Exercise 3

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
Induction
:::

### Factorial versus powers proof

::: {.worksheet-meta}
Base case first. Then write the inductive hypothesis in full before expanding the next factorial term.
:::

::: {.worksheet-grid}
::: {.worksheet-field}
### Base Case
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Inductive Hypothesis
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Inductive Step Working
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Final Induction Proof
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-check}
Self-check: did you show exactly why \((k+1)2^k > 2^{k+1}\)?
:::
:::

## Exercise 4

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
Find The Flaw
:::

### Horse-colour induction flaw

::: {.worksheet-meta}
The base case is fine. The failure happens in the inductive step when the overlap argument is supposed to carry the reasoning.
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Where Does The Argument Break?
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Rewrite The Explanation Clearly
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-check}
Self-check: did you explain why the step from \(n=1\) to \(n=2\) is the specific place the induction collapses?
:::
:::

## Exercise 5

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
Harder Proof
:::

### Rational number between two reals

::: {.worksheet-meta}
More precisely: if a is less than b, prove that there is a rational number strictly between them.

Hint: use the Archimedean property to choose a denominator so that one over that denominator is smaller than the gap, then choose a numerator that lands inside the interval.
:::

::: {.worksheet-grid}
::: {.worksheet-field}
### Key Property
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Strategy
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tight}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Working
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Final Proof
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::

::: {.worksheet-check}
Self-check: did you prove both inequalities \(a < m/n\) and \(m/n < b\) separately?
:::
:::

## Reflection

::: {.worksheet-block}
::: {.worksheet-kicker}
After The Work
:::

### Short Reflection

1. Which proof method felt most natural to you?
2. Where did your first draft become unclear?
3. What step was easiest to skip mentally but most important to write down?

::: {.worksheet-field}
### Notes
::: {.worksheet-lines .worksheet-lines--tall}
:::
:::
:::
